verdict_rationale: Two blockers are independently sufficient for NO-GO. First: the frontend audit-characteristics vocabulary diverges from the finalized 16a backend allowlist — three flags in 16a's canonical 13-member set are absent from 16b's metadata table, and one flag in 16b's table uses a name 16a's round-2 review explicitly corrected. The mismatch silently degrades the display for ~23% of the vocabulary. Second: removing `onCloseDrawer` from `PluginCardProps` without updating its sole production call site in `CatalogDrawer.tsx` will cause `tsc --noEmit` to fail; the plan's 'Expected: PASS' at Task 5 Step 5 is wrong, and the fix is not covered by 16c's scope.

findings.title: Frontend audit-characteristics vocabulary diverges from 16a canonical allowlist
findings.archetype: Tragedy of the Commons — each slice updates its own vocabulary; no mechanical gate keeps them in sync

evidence.prior_correction_missed: 16a review history (16a-phase-7a-backend.md:2323) explicitly states: 'Corrected test fixture emits_provenance → provenance to match 08-catalog-reshape.md authoritative vocabulary'. That fixup landed in 16a but 16b was never resynced.

failure_mode.silent: Backend emits flag strings 'provenance', 'retention', 'network' (all in 16a's allowlist, all authored on boundary plugins). Frontend lookupAuditCharacteristic() returns null for each. Renders as grey .audit-icon-unknown chips with raw flag text. The plan's forward-compatibility path was designed for future unknown flags; here it masks a present misalignment. No test failure — the 'unknown chip is forward-compatible' guard swallows the signal.
failure_mode.loud: None. The unknown-chip fallback is specifically designed to not crash, so this is a pure silent degradation path.
failure_mode.severity_justification: 23% of the vocabulary (3 of 13 members) renders incorrectly at staging smoke. The demo canonical test case (CSV source) annotated with 'provenance' and 'quarantine' as declared audit_characteristics in 16a Task 5 will show 'provenance' as a grey unknown chip, not its intended 'extra provenance' icon. Directly affects the operator's standard demo prompt.

findings.fix: In 16b Task 2 Step 3: (1) Replace flag 'emits_provenance' with 'provenance' throughout auditCharacteristics.ts and auditCharacteristics.test.ts. (2) Add entries for 'retention' and 'network'. The 16b reality review at line 1359 already made the network→external_call correction once; the provenance/retention gap is a second residual drift that was not caught in either of 16b's two reality reviews.

findings.title: Removing onCloseDrawer from PluginCardProps leaves CatalogDrawer.tsx with a TypeScript excess-property error
findings.category: Stranded call site — prop removed from interface, caller not updated
findings.archetype: Shifting the Burden — 16b removes the prop, 16c rewrites CatalogDrawer, neither plan explicitly owns the cleanup of the intermediate broken state

evidence.prop_removed_in: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:62-66 (scope statement), implemented in Task 5 Step 3 (new PluginCardProps interface has no onCloseDrawer)
evidence.catalogdrawer_not_in_16b_file_list: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:129-156 — CatalogDrawer.tsx is not in Modified list
evidence.16c_coverage_gap: 16c-phase-7c-frontend-integration.md:737-929 (Task 3 implementation) shows the CatalogDrawer rewrite but never renders <PluginCard> — the rewritten snippet only shows InlineChatSourceEntry and pluginList.map stubs. The onCloseDrawer cleanup at the call site is not explicitly stated anywhere in 16b or 16c.

failure_mode.silent: None. TypeScript excess-property check on JSX attributes rejects unknown props passed to a component interface. tsc --noEmit will fail.
failure_mode.loud: Task 5 Step 5 'npx tsc --noEmit' fails with: 'Type { ... onCloseDrawer: () => void; } is not assignable to type IntrinsicAttributes & PluginCardProps. Object literal may only specify known properties, and onCloseDrawer does not exist in type PluginCardProps'. Plan states 'Expected: PASS' — this is incorrect.
failure_mode.severity_justification: Compile failure blocks the Task-5 regression sweep from passing. If 16c is treated as the fix, the gap needs to be explicit in 16c's scope — currently it is not. Between 16b merge and 16c merge the frontend build is broken.

findings.fix: 16b Task 5 must also update CatalogDrawer.tsx line 359 to remove the onCloseDrawer prop from the <PluginCard> render, and add CatalogDrawer.tsx to the Task-5 commit. The plan currently says 'Files: Modify PluginCard.tsx, Modify PluginCard.test.tsx' — CatalogDrawer.tsx needs to be added to Task 5's file list. Alternatively, defer to 16c but make the 16c task description explicitly list the onCloseDrawer cleanup as a step.

findings.title: No mechanical parity gate between backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and frontend KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS
findings.category: Missing feedback loop — vocabulary drift has no detection circuit
findings.archetype: Limits to Growth — the vocabulary starts aligned, but each independent backend addition creates a drift opportunity with no circuit breaker

evidence.backend_gate: 16a-phase-7a-backend.md line ~1129: test_all_plugin_audit_characteristics_are_valid asserts declared strings are members of VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS
evidence.frontend_gap: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md Task 2 tests (lines 383-434) test that specific flags return non-null, but do NOT assert that KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS is a superset of the backend allowlist. The forward-compatibility 'null is valid' design masks the gap.

failure_mode.silent: When 16a adds a 14th characteristic to VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS, the frontend renders it as the grey unknown chip without any test failure. The drift accumulates silently across phases.
failure_mode.loud: None — the forward-compatibility path never crashes.
failure_mode.severity_justification: SYS-001 proves this scenario is not hypothetical: the drift has already happened in the current plan. Without a gate, it will recur. High severity because the failure is silent and the pattern is established.

findings.fix: Add a test to auditCharacteristics.test.ts that imports the backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS list (or a TypeScript-accessible mirror) and asserts KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS is a strict superset. If importing Python is not feasible in the frontend test environment, define a shared constant (e.g. a generated TypeScript file or a manually-maintained mirror constant) and add a CI check that compares it against the Python frozenset. A manual review note in the plan is insufficient — this needs a mechanical gate.

findings.title: TrustTierBadge semantic inversion risk: Tier 3 reads as 'worse' but could read as 'better' without tooltip context
findings.category: Cognitive model mismatch — badge semantics are anti-intuitive for non-technical users
findings.archetype: Implicit dependency on user mental model matching CLAUDE.md's three-tier framing

evidence.tooltip_mechanism: title attribute only (16b plan line 654-660 and TrustTierBadge implementation)
evidence.persona_risk: Linda persona (non-technical compliance user) does not hover tooltips reliably, does not understand the three-tier ELSPETH model, and will assign natural-language meaning to Tier 1/2/3 ordinals. 'Tier 3' sources can read as 'most capable', 'most complex', or 'most risky' depending on prior frame. CLAUDE.md §Data Manifesto frames tier 3 as 'external data, zero trust' — a negative connotation — while the badge itself is neutral.

failure_mode.silent: Non-technical users form wrong mental model; no test detects this.
failure_mode.loud: None in tests. Surfaces in user interviews or demo.
failure_mode.severity_justification: Medium: the tooltips are present and correct, and the badge CSS classes allow visual styling (trust-tier-badge-1/2/3) to reinforce the framing. This is recoverable via color treatment in the CSS pass without code changes.

findings.fix: In the CSS pass, ensure tier-3 styling does not use 'warning' or 'danger' red/yellow which would confirm the 'risky' misread. Use neutral or descriptive color treatment (e.g., a distinct border without a valence color). Consider replacing bare 'Tier 1/2/3' text with a short descriptor like 'External boundary' for tier 3. The design doc 08-§
uses '✓' and '⚠' iconography which carries its own valence — ensure that framing does not bleed into the tier badge.

findings.title: Removing PluginCard's onCloseDrawer and buildInsertionPrompt removes ALL action-from-catalog paths, but ChatInput.tsx comment still reads 'dispatched by PluginCard Use in pipeline action'
findings.category: Stale comment at integration point — misleads maintainers about event ownership

evidence.stale_comment: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/chat/ChatInput.tsx:63 — '// Listen for prefill events dispatched by PluginCard Use in pipeline action.'
evidence.new_dispatchers_in_16c: InlineChatSourceEntry.tsx (16c) becomes the only dispatcher after PluginCard.tsx stops dispatching

failure_mode.silent: The comment survives both 16b and (likely) 16c. A maintainer reading ChatInput.tsx six months from now infers that PluginCard dispatches prefill events and is confused why PluginCard.tsx has no such logic.
failure_mode.severity_justification: Medium: functional correctness is unaffected. The event contract still works. This is a maintenance debt item, not a functional bug. However, the ELSPETH CLAUDE.md instruction 'Comments are your institutional memory. Treat comments as load-bearing' elevates comment accuracy above normal priority.

findings.fix: 16b Task 5 Step 3 (or Step 6 commit) should update the ChatInput.tsx comment to describe the new dispatcher: 'Dispatched by InlineChatSourceEntry (catalog drawer) when the synthetic source entry is clicked.' The plan should add ChatInput.tsx to the files touched by Task 5.

findings.title: io_write attention-tone icon will flag every sink in the catalog as 'attention'
findings.category: Unintended reinforcing signal — visual tone designed for cautionary context applied uniformly

evidence.io_write_tone: 16b-phase-7b-frontend.md:517-520 — flag 'io_write', tone 'attention', tooltip 'Be careful — replay re-applies the side effects'
evidence.sinks_are_io_write: All sink plugins write I/O by definition; their audit_characteristics will include 'io_write' via the determinism derivation in 16a's _derive_audit_characteristics
evidence.visual_signal: Every plugin on the Sinks tab will carry a yellow/warning-style io_write chip

failure_mode.silent: User scanning the Sinks tab sees every entry carrying an attention icon. May interpret the Sinks tab as 'everything here is risky', reducing confidence in the catalog as a reference surface.
failure_mode.severity_justification: Low: the icon is accurate (sinks do write I/O; replay implications are real). This is a UX communication issue, not a correctness issue. The tooltip explains the semantics. Mitigated if CSS pass uses a muted rather than prominent attention color for io_write.

findings.fix: Consider either (a) demoting io_write to 'positive' tone with an informational framing ('writes output, replay-aware') rather than cautionary, or (b) treating io_write as expected-and-unremarkable for sinks and omitting it from the strip for sink-type plugins. This is a design decision for the CSS pass / 16c.

pattern_matches: Shifting the Burden (prop cleanup deferred without explicit ownership)
Implicit Dependency (frontend trusts backend vocabulary alignment without a gate)

blocking_issues.short: auditCharacteristics.ts metadata table diverges from 16a's canonical VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS — 'provenance', 'retention', 'network' absent; 'emits_provenance' present instead of 'provenance'. Silent render degradation for ~23% of vocabulary.

Removing onCloseDrawer from PluginCardProps without updating CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 breaks tsc --noEmit. Plan expects PASS at Task 5 Step 5; actual result is a TypeScript excess-property compile error.

warnings.short: No mechanical parity test between backend VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS and frontend KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS. The drift that created SYS-001 will recur on every backend vocabulary addition.
TrustTierBadge semantic inversion risk: bare ordinal labels (Tier 1/2/3) without icon-level framing may mislead non-technical personas. Mitigated by CSS pass color choices.

recommendations.short: Update ChatInput.tsx:63 comment in Task 5 to reflect that PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT is dispatched by InlineChatSourceEntry (16c), not PluginCard. Load-bearing comment per CLAUDE.md.
Consider demoting io_write tone from 'attention' to informational, or omitting it for sink-type plugins, to avoid flagging every Sinks-tab entry with a cautionary icon.

integration_point_stress.failure_modes: Silent render degradation for flags present in backend but absent in frontend table
integration_point_stress.plan_coverage: NO — forward-compatibility path masks the gap; no parity test exists
NO — CatalogDrawer.tsx not in 16b's modified file list; 16c does not explicitly own the cleanup

integration_point_stress.failure_modes: Event contract preserved correctly; comment becomes stale
integration_point_stress.plan_coverage: PARTIAL — event export stays, comment not updated

fix_surface.task_2_step_3: Replace 'emits_provenance' with 'provenance'; add 'retention' and 'network' entries to AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array. Update auditCharacteristics.test.ts test descriptions to match.
fix_surface.task_5_file_list: Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to Task 5 modified files; remove onCloseDrawer={onClose} from the <PluginCard> render at CatalogDrawer.tsx:359.
fix_surface.task_5_or_later: Update ChatInput.tsx:63 comment to name InlineChatSourceEntry as the new dispatcher.
fix_surface.future_gate: Add a vocabulary parity test in auditCharacteristics.test.ts (or a CI script) asserting KNOWN_AUDIT_FLAGS covers the full backend vocabulary.
